Ad Text:
AI Business Analyst

Role until June 2026 with 12 month extensions
Candidates must hold a current AGSVA Baseline Security Clearance or higher 
Flexible working arrangements available
 
About the company
This government agency is Australia’s economic regulator accelerating carbon abatement. They do this by administering national schemes that measure, manage, reduce and offset Australia’s carbon emissions, supporting transparent, responsible and effective climate action across the economy

 
About the role
The AI Business Analyst will build a deep understanding of business problems and data/AI capabilities, translating them into clear, testable user stories and acceptance criteria for multi disciplinary agile teams. You will shape AI enabled features and services that are ethical, transparent and compliant, and ensure the right human in the loop controls, explainability and auditability are in place across the AI lifecycle.

 
Duties

Elicit, analyse and translate business, data and AI requirements into epics, features and user stories, including non‑functional needs such as safety, transparency, robustness, latency, security and privacy.
Map processes and decision points to determine AI vs rules‑based logic; define human authority levels and HITL checkpoints; document model and prompt assumptions, guardrails and safety constraints.
Co‑design evaluation frameworks (ground truth, baselines, drift/fairness monitoring) and develop acceptance criteria and test plans for AI/ML features, including shadow testing, A/B trials, red teaming and rollback.
Partner with data scientists, engineers and product owners to convert outcomes into model objectives and data constraints; ensure alignment with Agency AI/Data Governance and support PIAs, TRAs and records management.
Lead workshops, discovery and backlog refinement; produce service blueprints and future‑state workflows; support change, comms and training for AI‑enabled processes and contribute to Communities of Practice.
 
Skills & experience


Minimum 3+ years’ experience as a BA/Systems Analyst in digital/service delivery environments, including agile teams
Hands‑on experience supporting AI‑enabled products in the Microsoft ecosystem (ML, NLP/LLMs, Copilot, intelligent automation) from discovery through delivery.
Proven ability to write testable user stories and acceptance criteria for AI features, including evaluation metrics and guardrails.
Working knowledge of responsible and secure AI practices (privacy by design, human oversight, explainability, bias mitigation) and strong stakeholder engagement/workshop facilitation skills.
Proficient with Azure DevOps and Miro (or equivalents), with excellent written and verbal communication that influences decisions through evidence and prototypes.
 
Benefits

Long term role 
Flexible working arrangements


How to apply

Candidates must be Australian Citizens with a current AGSVA Baseline Security clearance. 

If you would like to discuss further, please contact Megan Hudson - megan.hudson@calleo.com.au 
 
Calleo is an equal opportunity employer and we encourage applications from all people including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

Data Product Owner

Location – Macquarie Park, Sydney

Leadership role within our strong Data team, includes senior engagement across the business.
Join an ambitious firm which is growing, evolving & increasing its headcount.
Allied Credit has a hybrid work environment; we understand the value of a work-life balance.

Allied Credit is a leading vehicle finance firm with strategic alliances with global car, marine and motorcycle brands. Offering white label, joint venture and floorplan finance, our proprietary software provides a seamless, convenient and customer-focused finance service to consumers and businesses.

We are seeking a Data Product Owner to be responsible for the end-to-end delivery, operational performance and strategic uplift of Allied Credit’s data and analytics capabilities. The role leads Agile delivery, BAU work and project execution, and oversees the planning, prioritisation and governance of all data related work.

Reporting to the Head of Applications, this role manages Test and Business Analysts, while working closely with the Data Engineering team and the Data Architect to ensure data solutions are robust, secure and aligned to enterprise standards.

What you will be doing

Acting as the primary engagement point for all data and analytics requests in the Data team, overseeing all work pipelines and backlogs across BAU, projects and strategic initiatives.
Working with business and technology stakeholders to maintain the enterprise data roadmap, communicating progress, risks, issues and dependencies in a timely manner.
Supporting change management for new data capabilities, ensuring business teams are ready and supported.
Acting as Technical Project Manager for data centric initiatives, ensuring scope, timelines and risks are well managed, acting as Scrum Master where required.
Overseeing the stability, performance and reliability of enterprise data platforms, pipelines and analytics environments.
Maintaining disaster recovery readiness and compliance across all data environments.
Driving uplift in data quality, observability, documentation and operational processes.
Managing Test and Business Analysts, including performance management, coaching and workload coordination.
Partnering with the Data and Analytics Architect to embed modern design patterns, ensure architectural alignment and support long term data platform uplift.
Supporting the uplift of data governance, including data quality management, metadata, lineage, retention and stewardship frameworks.
Leading enterprise-wide data programs that support analytics transformation, automation, digital initiatives and AI readiness.
Overseeing vendor delivery performance for data platforms, engineering services and analytics tools.

Your background

Tertiary qualifications in IT, Computer Science, Data or a related field; relevant certifications (CSM, PSM, SAFe, PMP, PRINCE2) are desirable.
Strong experience with data platforms, data engineering, Azure services, CI/CD and DevOps practices.
Clear understanding of modern data architecture, data governance, API integration, security, privacy, lineage and DR.
Experience in financial services or other regulated environments would be beneficial but is not essential.
Demonstrated leadership across Agile delivery, cross functional teams and complex portfolios of work.
Exceptional ability to manage stakeholders and to communicate complex technical concepts in simple and practical terms.
Inclusive, supportive leadership style with strong stakeholder management skills.

Why work for Allied Credit?

Work-life balance - hybrid working arrangements allow a balance of working from home and the office, and we are always willing to discuss flexible working options.
Wellness day to take a day off for some R&R around your birthday and a Volunteer Day to support your community.
Discount offers on selected retailers, car loans and novated leasing options.
A learning environment where we take your development and career aspirations seriously.
16 weeks of paid parental leave along with Salary Continuance Insurance.

We want individuals who will buy into and thrive with Allied Credit’s Values of Customer Focus, Adaptability, Risk Confidence, Integrity and Teamwork.

Allied Credit pledges to treat all candidates with the utmost respect and professionalism throughout the recruitment process. We promise to conduct a fair, transparent, and inclusive hiring process, ensuring that all candidates are evaluated based on their skills, experience, and potential, regardless of race, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation or any other irrelevant characteristic.

Please contact Alex Kelly on 0484 109 354 for a confidential discussion. For further information on Allied Credit, please visit our website at https://alliedcredit.com.au/ or email hrservices@alliedcredit.net.au.

About Us

At Ubank, we’re here to help the digital generation get ahead financially. Think of us as your everyday money companion giving you a unified view of your finances, smart features, and real‑time insights. Whether it’s saving more, tracking your spending, or buying your first home sooner, ubank is built to help you succeed.

Why This Role Exists

You’re a data professional who thrives on enabling data‑driven decision‑making at scale. You bring a strong generalist toolkit and want to lead in areas like pipeline development, data modelling and deployment automation.
You’re energised by cutting‑edge technology and the opportunity to shape the future of analytics and insights for a fast‑growing, digitally native organisation.

Key Responsibilities

Connect and integrate data across our ecosystem to enable seamless data flows and support organisation‑wide decision‑making.
Partner with business stakeholders to translate data needs into well‑scoped, well‑understood work, focusing on both the what and the why.
Document designs, embed quality controls and uplift architectural maturity across the data team.
Define, map and model data flows from diverse systems in alignment with our target Data Vault architecture.
Build robust tests and quality checks for all new code and data assets, validating completeness and integrity.
Assess, adopt and implement new data sources, engineering techniques and platform capabilities.
Collaborate with technology teams, internal infrastructure partners and external vendors.
Work closely with Analysts to develop analytic models, curated datasets and business‑facing self‑service reporting assets.
Experience Required
Experience with data streaming technologies (e.g. Kafka, Flink).
Strong understanding of data modelling and warehousing, including hands‑on work with data lakes, relational databases, APIs, file storage, and modern lakehouse/cloud environments.
5+ years’ experience designing, developing and deploying complex datasets and pipelines, ideally including financial services data.
Deep hands‑on experience with data preparation, transformation and modelling.
Practical knowledge of CI/CD and Infrastructure‑as‑Code (Bitbucket, GitHub Actions, Jenkins or similar).
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with a proven ability to deliver data outcomes in partnership with technical and business teams.
Technical proficiency with SQL, Python, Java, Spark, PySpark and dbt.
A curious mindset with the ability to independently investigate and solve problems.
Desired Experience
Experience working with the Databricks platform (highly desirable).
Exposure to Growth & Marketing domains and Martech platforms.
Understanding of 3NF data modelling and its real‑world application.
Experience with AWS, Airflow, Fivetran, or cloud‑based PostgreSQL.
  Why Ubank?  
Innovation with impact: From meaningful work to amazing benefits, we equip you with the tools and support to drive real change - so your ideas don’t just stay ideas, they become impact. 
Collaborative, supportive & fun: We believe innovation is a team sport. It comes from a special kind of culture that’s highly collaborative and gives people the support they need to thrive. 
Build something great: Ubank is where ambition meets opportunity. We don’t just talk about change - we give you the tools, trust, and support to make it happen. 
Freedom to be yourself: We celebrate individuality and give you the flexibility to work in ways that suit your style. Our hybrid working model lets you balance connection and autonomy. 
Accelerate your Career: With clear paths for career development, your potential won’t just be recognised, it’ll be accelerated. 
  
We acknowledge the Traditional Custodians of our local area.   

This Sydney-based permanent role requires Australian or New Zealand citizenship or permanent residency.   

We welcome applicants of all backgrounds, and our Talent team is excited to connect with you.   
  
Apply now with your CV and explore this exciting opportunity!

Company Description


Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: 10th April 2026

Please note Rest does not accept speculative resumes from recruitment agencies 

Rest will review applications prior to the closing date and may close the role earlier 

Job Description


Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

This role serves as a strategic advisor, confidante and ‘right hand person’ responsible for amplifying the
efficiency and efficacy of Rest’s Chief Investment Officer (CIO). This role is pivotal in translating the CIO’s vision into an operational plan, facilitating decision-making, predicting risk and red flags before they occur, and ensuring the seamless execution of initiatives critical to the fund’s success.

The primary focus of the Head of CIO Office is to support the CIO to operate effectively with access to the right information at the right time to inform decision-making and drive outcomes aligned with the organisation’s strategic objectives and CIO’s agenda.

As a member of the Investment division’s Leadership Team, the Head of CIO Office will manage senior stakeholder relationships and provide leadership to key projects.

Key Responsibilities

Business Strategy: Lead the regular development and review of the Investment division’s strategicpriorities, including an actionable set of objectives, KPIs, projects, aspirational culture & behaviour change etc that are wholly aligned with the strategic direction of Rest overall.
Advisory and Decision Support: Serve as a thought advisor, offering the CIO and the Investment division’s Leadership Team perspectives they may not see based on data alone or from within individual teams. Prepare decision briefs; e.g. frame problems, develop options, consider risks and returns, manage stakeholder impacts, prepare recommendations etc.
Operating Model Execution: Work closely with the CIO to develop and implement the Investment division’s operating model and rhythm of management meetings. Be an important part of the ’connective tissue’ that binds the Investment division with other parts of Rest, especially where services are being provided to support the work of the Investment division and/or visa versa; e.g. Finance & Investment Operations, Data, Data, Technology & Delivery, Member (including Product), and Corporate Affairs (including Communications, Public Policy). Manage and set agendas, track progress, agree and follow up actions etc to ensure delivery of the strategic priorities.
Reporting: Prepare CIO reporting and other Rest Board and Committee papers, ensuring strategic alignment, quality written communication, timeliness, and consistency of message.
Communication – Internal and External: Work with the CIO and the Investment division’s Leadership Team, to communicate key priorities across the Investment division and Rest to keep stakeholders informed, updated, and aligned. Coordinate external communications for the CIO and the Investment division, including member / employer communications, external speaking / thought leadership pieces, and industry participation.

Qualifications


Qualifications

Business, Commerce, Economics, Law or Finance degree

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

Investment Management / Superannuation industry background highly desirable
Strong understanding of CIO role
High levels of integrity, discretion and diplomacy
Deep understanding of the roles and responsibilities of a professional superannuation trustee
Experience running a significant P&L
Superior written and verbal communication skills
Strong internal and external relationship builder
Demonstrable effectiveness when operating at executive and board-level

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities 
Hybrid working 
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave 
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave 
Linkedin Learning 
Income Protection Insurance 
Rest Excellence awards (peer recognition awards based on Rest’s values and behaviours) 
Rest Stops - meeting free breaks 

If you share our values, believe you can help make a difference for our members and want to be part of a leading superannuation fund with a Super culture, please click Apply Now.

Rest is committed to creating a flexible work environment and culture that embraces diversity, equity, and inclusion - where people feel welcome, safe to be themselves and inspired to do their best.

We value the different backgrounds, lived experiences and abilities our diverse team brings. We welcome and encourage applications from candidates of all ages, cultural backgrounds, faiths, gender identities, sexual orientations and thinking styles. This includes people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander peoples and those with disrupted work history due to career or other breaks.

Please note only people with the right to work in Australia will be considered.

*Funds under management as at 31 July 2025. Rest is recognised as a Responsible Investment Leader by the Responsible Investment Association Australia (RIAA) in its Responsible Investment Benchmark Report 2022.

Salary: Attractive salary with annual bonus opportunity

About Us

Established in 1946, Teys Australia is a beef processing and food production business. We are best known for providing quality Aussie beef to customers here in Australia and around the world. 

Teys Australia is part of the Cargill Food Enterprise and wholly owned by Cargill Inc.

With operations across 14 locations in Qld, NSW, Victoria, and South Australia, we are the second largest beef processor and exporter in Australia, employing over 5000 passionate people who drive our success, and bring their very best to work every day. 

Together, we focus on feeding people and enriching lives. Join us!

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced and forward-thinking Rewards & Workforce Insights Manager to help o elevate our remuneration governance and workforce analytics capability across the enterprise.

Reporting to the Enterprise General Manager People & Culture, this role plays a pivotal part in remuneration governance, ensuring market competitiveness and driving evidence‑based workforce analytics that support informed, enterprise‑wide decision making.

This is a unique opportunity for a commercially savvy remuneration and analytics specialist who loves turning data into insight and insight into action.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead remuneration governance, job evaluation, benchmarking and annual review cycles.
Deliver high‑quality workforce analytics, dashboards and predictive insights.
Partner with HR, Finance and Operations to model workforce costs and inform decisions.
Provide clear reward advice, reporting and data integrity oversight across the enterprise.

About YouEssential:

3–5+ years’ experience in remuneration, reward, workforce analytics or commercial HR.
Strong analytical capability with advanced Excel and data modelling skills (Tableau experience highly regarded).
Experience using Chris21(payroll), UKG, and Tableau reports
Experience with Korn Ferry (Hay Group) evaluations and salary benchmarking.
Confidence navigating enterprise agreements, awards or industrial frameworks.
Experience in blue‑collar, manufacturing or FMCG business highly regarded.
Commercial acumen, strong judgement and absolute discretion with sensitive data.

What We Offer

At Teys, we believe great leadership should be supported by meaningful rewards and a workplace where you can thrive. As part of our team, you will enjoy:

Competitive remuneration package + performance incentives
Unique annual meat allowance
Salary packaging options including devices and novated leasing
Health, wellbeing and continuity support
Injury/illness salary continuance scheme
Access to corporate discounts including Bupa healthcare
Genuine investment in your development and a clear pathway to future growth

To Apply

If you are looking for an opportunity to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment within a growing business offering excellent career development and opportunities, we encourage you to APPLY today. 

For a confidential discussion about this role, Luisa Hamze on 7 3198 9196. 

You can check us out at www.teysgroup.com.au or join us on https://www.facebook.com/TeysAustralia.

About the role 

As the General Manager, Emerging Businesses, you will be responsible for driving RACQ’s long term growth by identifying, testing and scaling new business opportunities that create value for members. You will lead the Emerging Business portfolio, building the organisational capability to explore new opportunities, run targeted experiments, and grow successful ventures to market scale. Working collaboratively across the organisation, you will ensure emerging initiatives are strategically aligned, grounded in member insights, supported by strong business cases, and managed within RACQ’s governance and risk frameworks. 

Key Responsibilities 

Lead and scale RACQ’s Emerging Business portfolio, shaping opportunities from exploration through to potential market launch.  
Develop gotomarket strategies, value propositions and partnerships informed by member insights and external market trends.  
Drive business experiments and build robust business cases to validate and prioritise future growth opportunities.  
Integrate emerging ventures into RACQ’s strategic, planning, product and governance frameworks in collaboration with internal stakeholders.  
Manage risks, financial metrics and organisational capability requirements to ensure the sustainable delivery of new business opportunities. 

About you 

You will have extensive senior leadership experience in identifying, trialling and scaling new business opportunities within complex or matrix environments.  
You excel in strategy development, decision making, test and learn, capability building and scaling opportunities. 
You are an exceptional communicator and influencer, with strong interpersonal skills and high EQ, able to build enduring relationships.  
You bring strong commercial acumen, strategic foresight, and the ability to build partnerships, influence Executive and Board forums, and foster a culture of innovation and continuous improvement. 
You have sound knowledge of risk, governance and regulatory frameworks, and experience ensuring compliance and effective controls.  
Proven people leader who builds high performing teams, develops talent and fosters a culture of collaboration, empowerment and accountability. 

Why join RACQ?

It is an exciting time to be part of RACQ as we continue our transformation towards better, smarter solutions that deliver more security and prosperity for Queenslanders. We are big enough to make a real difference in our community, yet small enough to nurture your professional and personal development.
By joining RACQ, you’ll be part of new sustainability initiatives, including a commitment to being a carbon neutral organisation by 2032. We’ve invested in and partnered with sustainable companies, including the introduction home solar and batteries, Green Home and Car Loans, and many other products and initiatives. We’ll continue to advocate for, research, and offer green solutions.

RACQ is committed to ensuring that every employee has access to flexibility in their role. We encourage all candidates to discuss what flexibility means to you, and how we can help you find your balance.

At RACQ we are passionate about building a diverse and inclusive workplace to create better outcomes for our employees and our members. We aim for our teams to be a true reflection of our communities and are committed to inclusion particularly people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage, and those who may experience diversity or disability related barriers in securing employment. Applicants from these and other diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. If you have any support or access requirement, please advise us at the time of applying.

What we offer you:

Our employee benefits are designed to enrich your work life, your home and the community you live in, some of these include:

Financial benefits and discounts on a range of banking, assistance and insurance products
Give back to our communities with paid volunteer leave, payroll giving and volunteering for community events such as MotorFest.
A range of flexible working and leave options, parental leave of 16 weeks primary carer leave, purchased leave up to 4 weeks per year, cultural observance leave, substitution of public holidays for cultural/religious reasons, personal leave for wellbeing, gender affirmation leave.
Prioritise your wellbeing with discounts on health programs and access to wellbeing initiatives such annual flu vaccinations, corporate health insurance offer and free and confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP). 
Invest in your development with in-house development programs and access to an extensive digital learning platform.

You can check out all our employee benefits at https://www.racq.com.au/about-us/careers/employee-benefits

Ready to apply to do good stuff daily?

Questions or require any adjustments? Please contact careers@racq.com.au or visit www.racq.com.au/about/careers/our-recruitment-process

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst - National Construction Platform
Ad Text:
Senior Finance Analyst - National Construction Platform
 
The Opportunity
A leading, privately owned construction and fitout group is seeking to appoint a Senior Finance Analyst to join its FP&A function.
 
Operating across multiple states with a national footprint, the business is in a significant growth phase and is currently preparing for a potential ASX listing. This role will play a key part in strengthening financial reporting, forecasting capability and governance as the business continues to scale.
 
Reporting to a Senior Finance Business Partner, you will gain direct exposure to senior leadership, including the CEO and Board, and contribute to decision-making across a complex, multi-divisional platform.
 
Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting & Close

Support monthly reporting, including preparation of executive and board-level finance packs
Deliver detailed actual vs forecast analysis with clear, decision-ready insights
Take ownership of components of the month-end close process
Assist with half-year and full-year statutory reporting
Forecasting & Planning

Support quarterly reforecasting processes across multiple divisions
Partner with FP&A leadership to improve forecasting accuracy and reporting quality
Incorporate operational and strategic inputs into financial planning
Commercial Engagement

Partner with Commercial Managers and General Managers across the business
Provide insight on revenue, cost performance, risks and opportunities
Translate financial data into clear, commercially relevant narratives
Governance & Controls

Support internal and external audit processes
Contribute to the uplift of reporting standards and controls in preparation for listing
Assist in strengthening governance frameworks across the finance function
Data & Analytics

Utilise advanced Excel, Power BI and Power Query for reporting and analysis
Work with large, complex datasets to improve reporting outputs
Drive efficiencies through automation and improved data processes
People & Capability

Provide guidance and support to junior finance team members
Act as a technical escalation point for reporting and data queries
About You

CA or CPA qualified
Strong experience in financial reporting, FP&A and forecasting within a complex environment
Advanced Excel capability, with experience in Power BI and Power Query
Confident engaging with senior stakeholders across finance and operations
Strong attention to detail, with the ability to provide clear commercial insight
Desirable:

Exposure to listed environments or IPO readiness
Experience within construction, infrastructure, or project-based businesses
Audit or external stakeholder exposure
Why This Role

Opportunity to join a large, nationally recognised platform in a period of growth
Direct exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision-making
Broad role combining technical reporting with commercial engagement
Involvement in IPO readiness and finance function uplift

About the Company
Step into a role where your analytical mindset, investigative curiosity and commitment to national security truly matter. This full‑time position places you at the centre of sensitive, high‑impact work supporting complex internal investigations. You’ll operate within a specialised, secure environment in Canberra, working onsite at designated locations and contributing directly to the protection of Australia’s most critical assets.
About the Role
As an Investigative Support Analyst, you will provide critical analytical and investigative support to a specialised insider‑threat team. You’ll interpret complex data to assist with sensitive internal investigations in a secure, detail‑focused environment, helping protect Australia’s national interests.
Responsibilities

Analyse diverse data sources to identify insights, patterns and relationships relevant to investigative activities.
Communicate findings clearly, connecting data to operational and contextual realities.

Ad Text:
At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

At ASIC, you can play a key role in shaping how innovation and emerging technologies are regulated in Australia's financial system. This Senior Specialist role sits within ASIC's FinTech team and contributes to the development of regulatory frameworks that respond to evolving market practices, technological change and innovation across financial services. As part of a developing leadership team, the role supports ASIC's response to new and complex regulatory challenges while maintaining strong consumer protection and market integrity outcomes.

With ASIC, you are choosing to be part of a collegial and intellectually rigorous organisation working on some of the most complex issues facing Australia's financial markets. This role offers the opportunity to work on novel and evolving matters where regulatory approaches are being shaped in response to innovation, emerging technologies and changing business models.

 

Melbourne or Sydney
Salary between $170,621 - $202,614 plus 15.4% superannuation
Two-year fixed-term contract
Applications close at 11:59pm AEDT, Wednesday 8 April 2026

 

The team 

ASIC's Markets Group safeguards trust and confidence in Australia's financial markets by overseeing the full regulatory lifecycle and ensuring market participants operate with integrity, resilience and transparency. Within the Group, the FinTech team leads ASIC's work on crypto and digital assets, tokenisation and decentralised finance, shaping regulatory policy through law reform, guidance development, and close engagement with industry and internal stakeholders.

 

The role

As a Senior Specialist in the FinTech team, you will provide strategic advice, analysis and expertise across complex regulatory and policy workstreams associated with financial innovation and emerging technologies. You will work closely with senior leaders as ASIC continues to evolve its regulatory approach in this area. You will be accountable for:

Providing strategic analysis and advice on highly complex regulatory matters, including legislative and law reform initiatives and the development of regulatory frameworks relevant to innovative and evolving financial services
Providing guidance and support to ASIC staff on emerging products and services, including the underlying technologies and business models
Supporting executive‑level engagement, including with the Commission, on innovation‑driven market developments, regulatory priorities and associated risks
Contributing to the development of supervisory, enforcement and change management strategies to support implementation of new or evolving regulatory regimes
Driving targeted initiatives that advance responsible innovation and the modernisation of Australia's financial system
Developing and maintaining effective relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including representing ASIC in government, regulatory or industry forums
Embedding the use of data, regulatory intelligence and technical analysis to support evidence‑based decision making and continuous improvement

 

About you

You bring substantial experience in the fintech industry, financial services, financial markets or a regulatory environment, with a strong understanding of technology, policy, risk and compliance issues. Your experience may have been gained through government policy roles, regulatory settings, a fintech firm or legal practice, and you are able to translate complex reform, innovation or emerging technology developments into practical regulatory outcomes. You have strong analytical and research skills, experience providing technical advice to senior decision‑making forums, and the ability to manage complex work involving multiple stakeholders. Relevant tertiary qualifications are required.

You communicate clearly and effectively with a wide range of audiences and work collaboratively across organisational boundaries. You manage competing priorities, take responsibility for delivering outcomes, and apply sound judgement in ambiguous and evolving environments. You demonstrate strong problem‑solving and risk management capability, value evidence‑based decision making, have strong communication skills and model ASIC's values of accountability, professionalism and teamwork.

 

View the position description for more information or click ‘apply' to start your application.

Applications close at 11:59pm AEDT, Wednesday 8 April 2026

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms.

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander decent are encouraged to apply.

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

 

To work with us, you need to be an Australian citizen and be prepared to complete an ASIC Suitability and Baseline Assessment.

ResourcesWA is a boutique consulting firm specialising in bridging the technical and financial aspects of mining project development. We are dedicated to delivering strong commercial outcomes for the owners and sponsors of power and mineral projects, providing high-quality services that help clients navigate complex challenges, make high quality decisions, optimise operations, and drive sustainable growth. We operate across the full asset lifecycle - from early concept studies through to existing operations.

We are now looking to expand our commercial capability with a Commercial & Delivery Lead position. This senior level roles oversees the financial and commercial performance of projects across their lifecycle, ensuring alignment between project delivery, client outcomes, and ResourcesWA’s commercial objectives. This position will play a key role in our future growth, proving commercial leadership internally and outwardly supporting client engagement and business development.

The Role:

Work with the technical team to interpret financial aspects of projects, providing insight into risks, value drivers and commercial trade-offs

Create a capital market view of current and future clients and their markets through financial and peer analysis and benchmarking

Develop project estimates and financial models in collaboration with the technical team and provide value in differentiating business cases for optionality within projects

Develop and present high-quality business cases to executive teams and clients

Identify areas of financial and commercial risk and develop mitigation strategies

Oversee the financial performance of projects throughout delivery, ensuring alignment to budgets, forecasts, and commercial assumptions

Monitor project progress against financial models, identifying variances and advising on corrective actions

Support program-level planning and prioritisation, ensuring optimal allocation of resources across projects

Ensure commercial governance is maintained across projects, including scope control, change management, and margin protection

Engage with clients at a strategic level to ensure project outcomes align with their financial and operational objective

Develop existing relationships and create new opportunities across the Energy, Renewables and Mining sectors

Maintain awareness of market trends and opportunities, advising the executive team on leads and opportunities

Represent ResourcesWA at industry and networking events and provide introductions and business development opportunities through industry networks

Requirements:

Degree in Engineering, Project Management, Construction Management or similar

5 + years’ experience in commercial role within mining, energy or infrastructure

Ability to think strategically and align day-to-day operations with broader company objectives

Skilled at managing relationships and expectations among a variety of stakeholders

Capability to identify, analyse, and plan for risks in both existing projects and potential new ventures

High-level communication and stakeholder management skills

Ability to manage ambiguity and competing priorities

Post graduate qualification or training in financial modelling and analysis

The size of our organisation means exposure to a diverse range of projects and a broader set of end-to-end tasks in your role, offering an ideal opportunity for professional growth. If you are someone with a sound financial foundation, keen to work in client facing, senior level role that is not just modelling, apply today or reach out to Sara Scarboro for more information on 0400 789 144  

Please note that applicants with full Australian working rights will be considered, thank you for your understanding.

Job Title: Sr. Business Analyst - Process and Change - NV1 Clearance Required
Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst - Process & Change - NV1 Cleared

Location: Melbourne
Work Type: Contract
Security Requirement: NV1 Clearance (Mandatory)
Sponsorship: Not available for this position

Role Purpose
 
Within a complex ICT delivery program, the Senior Business Analyst - Process & Change leads the discovery, analysis, and definition of complex business requirements. You will design improved business processes to enable effective solution delivery, maintaining a strong end-to-end perspective from problem definition through to implementation readiness. This role partners closely with change management to ensure successful adoption.
 
Key Responsibilities
 
Business Analysis

Lead end-to-end discovery to understand business problems, objectives, and constraints.
Elicit, analyse, validate, and document complex business, functional, and non-functional requirements.
Translate business needs into clear user stories, use cases, and acceptance criteria.
Maintain requirements traceability through design, build, test, and implementation.
Provide options analysis and recommendations to support informed business decisions.
Process Analysis & Design

Lead current-state (as-is) analysis to identify inefficiencies, risks, and control gaps.
Design future-state (to-be) processes aligned to business strategy and customer outcomes.
Map processes using agreed standards (e.g., BPMN), including roles, handoffs, and controls.
Define operational impacts, measures, and success criteria for new processes.
Ensure processes are practical, implementable, and ready for transition to BAU.
Stakeholder Engagement & Change Support

Build strong relationships with senior stakeholders, SMEs, and delivery teams.
Facilitate complex workshops to drive alignment and project outcomes.
Provide high-quality inputs to change impact assessments and inform training/readiness activities.
Support engagement with stakeholders to explain upcoming process and system changes.
Skills & Experience
 
Essential

Must hold a current NV1 Security Clearance.
Significant experience delivering complex change within large-scale environments.
Strong requirements elicitation and documentation capability.
Demonstrated leadership in business process analysis and design.
Excellent facilitation skills and the ability to work through ambiguity.
Australian Citizenship is required (No sponsorship available).
Desirable

Experience supporting change initiatives or familiarity with frameworks (e.g., ADKAR, Prosci).
Experience in Agile or hybrid delivery environments.
Attributes

Outcome-focused, pragmatic, and proactive.
Comfortable operating at both strategic and detailed levels.
Strong sense of ownership and accountability.

Come and join us in building the infrastructure our planet needs to achieve a sustainable future.

The Opportunity

We're seeking a Process Lead who will act as the vital bridge between the business and the Construction Futures team, uncovering challenges and shaping ideas that enhance operational performance and digital capability. You'll work closely with teams across the organisation to identify opportunities in process optimisation, data improvement, and disruptive innovation, guiding stakeholders from problem discovery through to early solution design.

About the Australian Automobile Association
 
The Australian Automobile Association (AAA) is exclusively partnering with HorizonOne Recruitment to recruit a Senior Data Analyst. Data sits at the heart of how AAA operates and delivers impact, representing the interests of millions of members and supporting grassroots campaigns across Australia.

AAA plays a key role in shaping public debate and influencing policy outcomes through credible, trusted data and insights.
 
You’ll be joining a highly collaborative, low-turnover environment with a flat structure and open access to leadership. Ideas are shared openly and tested quickly. This is the kind of place where you can raise an idea in the morning and be working on it that afternoon.
 
The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to own and shape the end-to-end data function within an organisation that genuinely values the role data plays in influencing real-world outcomes.

Own end-to-end data and analytics across the organisation
Freedom to define problems and build solutions your way
Clear purpose focused on delivering outcomes that support the greater good
Create data products used across a wide range of audiences, including Board and Ministerial level
The Role

As the Senior Data Analyst, you’ll own and operate across the full data lifecycle from sourcing and shaping raw data through to insight-led reporting and storytelling that informs and supports advocacy, policy, and communications.
Key responsibilities include:

Sourcing, validating, and integrating data from multiple inputs (ABS, government, industry, aggregators) 
Translating data into clear, compelling insights that explain what’s happening, why it matters, and how it can be used
Identifying new opportunities to slice, analyse, and present data in different ways for varied audiences
Developing and maintaining dashboards and reporting using SQL and Power BI
Owning and improving data processes, while mentoring a junior analyst and supporting BAU when required
The Ideal Person

You’re an experienced data professional who enjoys being hands-on and owning data solutions end-to-end, particularly in environments where the path isn’t predefined.
 
You’re naturally curious and proactive  the type of person who says “I’ve done this, and here’s what else we could do…”  or "I found this interesting insight from the data and I think it would be impactful to do x with it …" rather than waiting for direction.
 
You likely:

Have strong hands-on experience and can also operate with a broader, more strategic lens
Are comfortable with ambiguity and enjoy figuring things out from first principles
Take a methodical approach and pride yourself on quality, accuracy, and rigour
Enjoy working across a business, engaging stakeholders, and shaping how data is used
You bring:

Why your role matters

The Marketing & Digital Experience (M&DX) portfolio has been established to enhance UNSW's global brand reputation, create sharper brand distinctiveness, amplify UNSW's globally renowned expertise across Societal Impact Framework themes, and support the University to realise the ambition of its 'Progress for All' strategic plan.

Striving to be at the forefront of marketing and digital experience leading practice, M&DX instils audience centricity and leverages UNSW's enterprise technology investment, including but not limited to Adobe Experience Platform, Sales CRM, and emerging AI capabilities, to deliver deeper personalised relationships through sophisticated marketing, content, data, digital experience, and student recruitment functions in support of UNSW's commercial and societal impact outcomes.

As the Manager, Customer Analytics Transformation you will step into this newly created role where you will focus on the support of UNSW's lead management ecosystem and 'Progress for All' strategic ambitions. You will primarily be responsible for data strategy and governance, digital innovation and adoption, and benefit realisation to drive transformational enterprise-wide customer experience outcomes.

About You:  

This role will suit a strategic and forward-thinking leader who can drive the evolution of UNSW’s customer analytics capability, leveraging data, AI and digital innovation to deliver personalised, insight-driven experiences across the student, alumni and research lifecycle. More specifically you must have:

Provide operational leadership and manage the Customer Analytics transformation and innovation area and its team members.
Position M&DX as the Centre of Excellence for lead management and digital personalisation. Working collaboratively with marketing, sales and digital experience leads on the development and implementation of UNSW's data-and-technology-driven CX/DX innovation strategy and roadmap in alignment with 'Progress for All' and M&DX priorities.

Responsible for development and maintenance of M&DX AI models, governance and architecture. Identify and facilitate prioritisation and development of high-value use cases for the customer analytics management ecosystem that demonstrate tangible business outcomes across student recruitment, alumni engagement, and research partnerships.
Partner on the strategic roadmap definition for lead management transformation, conducting horizon scanning to identify emerging trends and technologies that position UNSW as a sector leader.

Enable customer journey strategies across all MDX priority audiences, driving the evolution from campaign-based to always-on lifecycle marketing approaches.
Foster innovation culture through experimentation and test-and-learn approaches, mentoring M&DX team members on customer analytics best practice.
Build and maintain a working relationship with UNSW stakeholders to advocate for customer-centric approaches and to ensure alignment and seamless integration.

About Us

UniSuper is a workplace where highly skilled professionals are empowered to Think Great and deliver exceptional retirement outcomes for our members. Are you ready to take on new challenges, drive excellence and shape UniSuper's success? Join UniSuper as a leader and empower your team within an industry leading culture, driving simplification and performance.

The Technology & Delivery team ensures the sustained health and maintenance of UniSuper's core systems and platforms and partners with the business to plan and deliver technology change that supports scale, simplification and execution of UniSuper's strategy.

This is a pivotal leadership role where you will shape how change is planned, governed and delivered across UniSuper, enabling better decisions, stronger execution and meaningful outcomes for our members.

The role

The key focus of this role is enabling successful portfolio execution across Technology & Delivery through clear governance, insight and delivery support. You will provide portfolio‑level visibility, decision support and operating rhythm while continuously improving how UniSuper plans, funds and governs change. You will lead the evolution from a traditional PMO to a modern, enabling and outcome‑focused enterprise portfolio function.

You will be accountable for:
Shaping and leading the enterprise portfolio operating model to support effective planning, funding and delivery of change
Providing portfolio‑level oversight, insights and assurance across programs and projects, including risks, dependencies, progress and benefits
Leading and developing a high‑performing team that enables delivery excellence across Technology & Delivery
Establishing and overseeing fit‑for‑purpose governance that balances regulatory, financial and risk obligations with pace and agility
Partnering closely with the Chief Technology Officer and senior leaders to support decision making, prioritisation and execution
Overseeing portfolio financials, forecasting and benefit tracking to ensure alignment to strategic priorities

About you

You are a modern enterprise portfolio leader who brings strong experience in traditional program management and enjoys reshaping those practices into contemporary delivery models. You are comfortable operating at a portfolio and enterprise level, influencing senior leaders and creating clarity in complex environments. You value outcomes, transparency and continuous improvement and take pride in enabling others to deliver their best work.

You bring:


Proven experience leading an enterprise portfolio or Project Management Office in a complex organisation
Strong leadership and stakeholder management capability, with the ability to coach and influence at senior levels
Experience enabling modern delivery practices, governance and portfolio planning cadences
Sound financial and commercial acumen, including oversight of budgets and forecasting
Strong communication, analytical and systems thinking skills
A result focused, accountable and outcome driven mindset

What We Offer

UniSuper believes that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office. UniSuper is proud of our culture and benefits, which empower our people to achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:


Flexible working arrangements
Annual Bonus and Competitive Remuneration
Strong focus on learning, development and leadership growth
Competitive superannuation up to 17%
Wellbeing initiatives and support

It should go without saying, but at UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion. We believe that a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don't meet all the requirements.

If you feel you have the experience and skills required for this role, please apply.


Think Great. Create Great.

High-growth services business

Sydney-based with international operations
Broad portfolio of growth and transformation projects

About the Company

This is a fast moving and ambitious services business which has grown successfully over many years and is now embarking on the next phase of development. With an experienced leadership team, they have multiple projects underway that will set them up for substantial growth over the next few years. Therefore this role will be a key member of a small senior team who can add value across the business by leveraging their broad project planning and execution skills and embedding that discipline across the organisation.

About the Role

This will be the first dedicated Project Manager role and will oversee the end-to-end delivery of varied cross-functional initiatives across this rapidly scaling business. This is a hands-on role suited to someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, enjoys solving complex problems, and can bring structure and clarity to ambiguous situations. You will work closely with teams across Product, Technology, Operations, Finance, Legal and Sales to plan, coordinate and successfully deliver key initiatives. Reporting to the CFO, you’ll play an important role in improving how the business delivers change — introducing practical project management disciplines and frameworks while ensuring teams remain aligned, accountable and focused on outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Project Planning & Execution

Lead the planning and delivery of cross-functional initiatives such as  technology projects, business integration and process improvements
Define project scope, milestones, timelines and success measures with stakeholders
Identify and manage risks, issues and dependencies
Ensure projects are delivered on time and aligned with business goals
Stakeholder Engagement

Work closely with leaders across the business to drive collaboration and alignment
Communicate project progress, risks and decisions clearly to stakeholders, including senior leaders
Manage competing priorities and negotiate realistic delivery timelines
Reporting

Introduce practical project management practices and governance to support effective delivery
Maintain clear documentation, reporting and decision tracking
Implement appropriate PM tools and methodologies
Resource & Budget Management

Determine resource requirements and allocate across projects appropriately
Monitor budgets and staffing plans
Work closely with CFO and finance team on forecast costs and financial accountability
  
About You

You will be comfortable working across different cultures in overseas offices and can quickly adjust to changing priorities. You should be pragmatic, collaborative and confident bringing structure to varied projects, while maintaining a strong focus on delivery outcomes.

Several years of experience in project management or a similar role delivering cross-functional initiatives
Experience working in large organisations but looking for a broader role in a growing, mid-size business
Proven ability to manage multi-million dollar budgets and perform financial analysis
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Comfortable bringing structure to ambiguous or evolving environments
Strong command of project management tools and methodologies
Project and Change Management certifications (PRINCE2, PMP, Agile/Scrum, Six Sigma or similar)

About your Role

The Data Governance Manager is accountable for strengthening TMBL’s Data Governance position by assisting with the uplifting and embedding of a fit-for-purpose Data Governance framework across the organisation, including governance, policies, training, reporting and assurance. You will support the strategic development and implementation of data solutions that address regulatory requirements including Economic and Financial Statistics (EFS) reporting and Open Banking compliance.
  
You will support the Bank’s data governance agenda, ensuring robust management of critical data elements (CDEs), clear data ownership, documented data lineage, and effective data retention practices. You will act as an escalation point for Data Governance issues and incident management processes and monitor and support the implementation of Data Governance-related regulatory changes and best practices across TMBL.

Key Responsibilities

Provide timely, high-quality, risk-based Data Governance advice on complex matters, including technology change and AI initiatives.
Collaborate with enterprise risk and compliance teams to align regulatory reporting with broader risk management frameworks and support the Risk team through reporting and analytical solutions.
Act as an escalation point to assess Data Governance incidents, coordinate investigation and response, and support regulatory notifications.
Operationalise and continuously improve TMBL’s Data Governance framework, driving pragmatic governance, reporting, assurance and continuous compliance to drive data governance framework uplift.
Develop/maintain Data Governance policies, standards, procedures and Regulatory change oversight: Track upcoming Data Governance regulatory changes, translate obligations into business requirements, and support implementation and uplift activity.
Data Quality & Assurance – Drive initiatives to monitor and improve the quality, integrity, and defensibility of regulatory and risk-related data.
Training & culture: Design and deliver Data Governance training and communications to build capability and embed a strong Data Governance culture.
About You

As the successful  candidate you will showcase: 

Demonstrated experience in Data Governance roles within financial services, including managing critical data elements (CDEs), data lineage, ownership, and retention.
Background in data quality management and assurance, especially in highly regulated reporting environments.
Strong stakeholder management and partnership experience across cross-functional teams (e.g., Risk, Compliance, Technology, Customer, Legal, Cyber, Privacy, Product, Operations) to deliver integrated data solutions.
Experience in developing and/or updating Data Governance Policies, Standards & Procedures.
Hands-on experience designing and implementing data systems and solutions that meet compliance and reporting standards.
Experience with Data Management tooling such as Alation, Collibra or similar.
Working knowledge of CPG235, BCBS239, and CPS230.
  
Skills and Qualifications
  

Relevant Tertiary Qualification in Commerce, Economics, Finance, Computer Science or Similar
Strong and extensive experience in a similar data governance management  role working within a major Australian Bank
Professional Certification in Data Governance DCAM- must have
Professional certifications in data governance compliance or risk management (e.g., DCAM, DAMA-DMBOK, CISA, CRISC)- highly desirable.
Training or experience in data governance, risk/compliance, technology, cyber, or AI governance.

Teachers Mutual Bank Limited is one of the largest mutual banks in Australia. We are a globally recognised socially responsible bank with profit-for-purpose as our business model and philosophy. At our Bank, social responsibility is built-in, not bolted on and drives our business practices, people, and products. Join Us at a Defining Moment

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business -responsibly and at speed.

But we’re just getting started. As we scale into our next phase of growth, we’re looking for senior, seasoned consultants who don’t just want to deliver great solutions but help shape the future of data, governance, and AI capability.


Our missing person...

We’re looking for a Data Governance (Senior Consultant) who can help our clients turn governance into a genuine business enabler across data, analytics, and AI. You’ll work across people, process, and technology to design, implement, and sustain governance programs that improve trust in data, strengthen compliance, and enable responsible AI adoption.

This role is perfect for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy and delivery - comfortable advising executives one moment and working hands-on with data teams the next.

You’ll be highly client-facing in this role, acting as a trusted advisor who can challenge constructively, push back where needed, and help clients make pragmatic governance decisions that balance control with delivery.

This role also requires a strong coordinator who can bring together business, data and risk stakeholders to drive alignment, maintain momentum, and ensure governance initiatives translate into practical outcomes.

Key Responsibilities
Design and implement enterprise data governance frameworks, policies, standards, and operating models aligned to client goals and industry best practice.
Define the Enterprise Data Governance model and operating rhythm to underpin AI, analytics, and broader data transformation initiatives.
Establish minimum standards for AI-enabling data, including data quality, lineage, metadata, access, and accountability.
Map AI governance requirements to existing data governance artefacts, controls, and business processes.
Contribute to the AI Governance Framework and Operating Model, ensuring alignment between responsible AI practices and enterprise data foundations.
Define and document data ownership, stewardship, and accountability models across business and technology functions.
Provide strong day-to-day coordination across client stakeholders, ensuring governance decisions, actions, and dependencies are managed effectively across business and technical teams.
Support the establishment of data quality management processes, metrics, controls, and monitoring mechanisms.
Facilitate workshops, stakeholder forums, and training sessions to drive adoption, engagement, and cultural change around data ownership, literacy, and responsible AI.
Collaborate with client leadership to improve decision-making, regulatory compliance, business performance, and confidence in AI and analytics outcomes.


Requirements



6+ years’ experience in data governance, data management, information management, or consulting roles.
Experience developing and implementing data governance policies and procedures within complex organisational environments.
Strong understanding of data governance principles, frameworks, and best practices, including familiarity with DAMA-DMBOK and related governance disciplines.
Proven experience designing or implementing governance frameworks, policies, standards, stewardship models, and operating models.
Experience supporting AI and digital transformation initiatives through strong data governance foundations.
Strong understanding of the foundational data capabilities required to enable AI, including data quality, metadata, lineage, access controls, and governance by design.
Experience mapping governance requirements across business, risk, compliance, and technology stakeholders.
Familiarity with metadata management, data cataloguing, and lineage tools such as Collibra, Informatica, Alation, or similar platforms.
Exposure to data quality management processes, controls, and tooling.
Strong consulting and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to advise senior leaders while also working closely with delivery teams.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills - able to translate governance concepts into practical business value for both executive and operational audiences.
Comfortable working in multi-disciplinary teams spanning strategy, engineering, analytics, and AI.


Benefits



We’re not just delivering AI and data projects.. we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer opinions and ideas.

You’ll join a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence - in your projects, your practice, and the broader business. And as we grow, so will you. Here’s what that looks like:

Grow With Purpose

Structured development pathways so you always know what you’re working toward.
Paid certifications across all major cloud and data technologies (mastery matters)
Weekly training and knowledge exchanges where practitioners share real project insights.


Support That Helps You Thrive

A dedicated support lead to mentor, guide and help you navigate your career
Regular 360° feedback loops, giving you clear direction and honest insight into your progress.


A Community You’ll Enjoy Being Part Of

Innovation days where you can experiment, prototype and sharpen new skills.
Team days, social catchups,  many new community clubs…
Opportunities to lead, influence and shape our growing practice as we expand across the region.


If you want to be part of a team that cares just as much about how we deliver as what we deliver; where curiosity is valued, ideas matter, and your impact is felt - drop us a message and start the conversation today.

Salary: AUD 120 - 130 per hour, Negotiable hourly rates.

Whitehaven Coal is the dominant player in Australia's only emerging high-quality coal basin. With a culture based on our STRIVE values, our reputation for excellence in project delivery and safe, efficient and environmentally responsible operations continues to grow.

Our STRIVE Values connect us and define how we behave. Whitehaven Coal award, recognise and celebrate individuals and teams going above and beyond the requirements of their role to deliver excellent outcomes while demonstrating our values of Safety, Teamwork, Respect, Integrity, Value and Excellence.





About the Role

The Improvement & Analytics team within Whitehaven's Remote Operating Centre (ROC) in Brisbane has an exciting opportunity for a Specialist - Data Analytics to play a key role in shaping the future of our operations.

This position places you at the forefront of innovation in mining, directly influencing how our assets operate today and into the future.
You will have opportunities to expand your technical capability, work on complex challenges, and contribute to a high‑performing team focused on excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.

Reporting to the Superintendent - Improvement & Analytics, you will support our operating assets in making informed, data‑driven decisions through ongoing collaboration, maintaining the integrity of production data, and optimising key processes across the value chain.


About Whitehaven's Remote Operating Centre (ROC)

Whitehaven's Brisbane‑based ROC is a major driver of our digital transformation and modern mining strategy.
The ROC integrates advanced operational technology, real‑time data, and innovative digital tools to optimise the performance of our mining operations.

Here, teams work with high‑quality data streams, market leading analytics platforms, and emerging technologies to support safer, more efficient, and more predictable operations.
You will join a collaborative, high‑performance environment that leverages data, automation, and innovation to deliver continuous improvement across our business.
The ROC offers a unique opportunity to influence the future of mining by shaping how intelligence, insights, and digital capability are applied across our operations.



What You Will Do

• Validate data quality and develop innovative solutions to reduce the administrative effort associated with manual reconciliation.
• Develop dashboards, visualisations, and actionable insights using tools such as SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Power BI, PI Vision, SSRS, and related platforms.
• Build digital products using Fabric, Power Automate and other Microsoft 365 tools.
• Create and maintain clear documentation for data workflows to support consistent and efficient processes within the Data & Analytics team.
• Act as a liaison between on‑site operations and the Data & Analytics team, providing insights and resolving data‑related queries to enable informed decision‑making.

What You Will Bring

• Intermediate understanding of key mining processes and the mining value chain.
• Experience with analysing and interpreting data from fleet management systems and washplant source systems.
• Demonstrated advanced proficiency in SQL, DAX, Power BI, and related languages

• Demonstrated experience with data reconciliation, reporting, and document management.
• Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, with the ability to draw meaningful insights from data.
• Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with both on‑site and off‑site stakeholders.

Highly Regarded Skills

Candidates with experience or interest in the following areas will be viewed favourably, as these capabilities align with the ongoing digital and analytical evolution of our ROC:



• Data Science - statistical analysis, optimisation, data modelling, or analytics‑driven process improvement.
• Data Engineering - ETL processes, data pipelines, cloud data platforms, or database optimisation.
• Automation & Scripting - Python, R, or automation tools to reduce manual work and streamline data processes.
• Power BI & Visual Analytics - development of dashboards, visualisations, and insights for a range of stakeholders.
• Operational Technology (OT) Data - experience with sensor data, real‑time systems, historian databases (e.g., PI), or telemetry‑based monitoring.
• Process Optimisation - exposure to Lean, Six Sigma, or continuous improvement frameworks supported by data‑driven insights.

This is a unique opportunity to join Gordon Legal’s Class Actions Team in a senior operational role supporting complex class action and settlement scheme work.

We are seeking a high‑calibre Project Leader/Business Analyst to play a central role in the delivery, coordination and ongoing performance of large‑scale class actions and court‑approved settlement schemes. This role sits at the intersection of law, operations, data and external stakeholder management.

About Gordon Legal

Gordon Legal is a progressive, medium‑sized law firm with a national reputation for running significant and high‑profile class actions.

Our size and structure mean minimal bureaucracy — ideas move quickly, initiative is encouraged, and people are trusted to take ownership of their work. Our teams work closely with the Partnership group, who remain hands‑on and deeply involved in both legal strategy and operational delivery. Collaboration, accountability and problem‑solving are central to how we work.

The Role

This is a dedicated project management role within the Class Actions team, supporting litigation and court‑approved settlement scheme administration.

The role will formally sit within the scheme administration structure and will work closely with partners, senior lawyers, internal teams and multiple external stakeholders.

Key responsibilities include:

Project managing large and complex class action and settlement scheme programs

Acting as a central coordination point between internal teams and external parties (including other law firms, administrators and regulators)

Managing timelines, deliverables, dependencies and risk across programs of work

Overseeing and improving systems, processes and workflows to support scheme delivery

Producing and maintaining clear reporting, dashboards and updates on progress, risks and performance

Supporting data‑driven workstreams, including reporting, reconciliation and scheme administration inputs

Identifying downstream impacts of operational changes and proactively managing those impacts

Reducing the operational and coordination burden on legal teams

This is a highly visible, hands‑on role with real responsibility and influence across major matters.

About You

We are looking for someone who brings strong project management capability and is comfortable operating in complex, fast‑moving environments.

You may come from a legal or non‑legal background. What matters most is your skill set and approach.

You will ideally have:

Strong project management experience (legal, professional services, government, regulatory or complex programs)

Excellent stakeholder management skills, including dealing with demanding external parties

The ability to think in systems and processes and anticipate downstream impacts

Confidence coordinating multiple workstreams and priorities

Strong communication skills and the ability to translate complexity into clear, practical actions

Comfort producing reports, tracking progress and working with data at a practical level

Experience in class actions, settlement schemes, legal project management or business analysis will be highly regarded, but is not essential.

Why Join Us

Work on nationally significant class actions with real social and financial impact

A rare opportunity to sit at the centre of large‑scale settlement scheme delivery

Direct access to partners and senior decision‑makers

An entrepreneurial, low‑hierarchy environment

A role with genuine scope to grow into broader scheme administration leadership

Competitive remuneration commensurate with experience

Apply

Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:


Help shape the future of Australia's largest UKG practice
Join a national community of 90+ UKG specialists within Deloitte's Human Capital practice
Work on complex workforce transformation programs with some of Australia's most recognised organisations

About the Team

Deloitte's UKG practice is the largest in Australia and continues to grow rapidly. Our team brings together specialists who design and deliver workforce technology solutions that help organisations better manage, schedule and support their people.

Joining Deloitte means working on large-scale UKG implementations that transform how organisations operate. Our teams deliver across industries including health, retail, supply chain, hospitality and the public sector, solving complex workforce challenges and enabling organisations to operate more effectively.

You'll work across the full UKG ecosystem, supported by specialists in timekeeping, advanced scheduling, forecasting, analytics, reporting and integrations.

What sets this team apart is the depth of expertise around you. With more than 90 UKG specialists across Australia, and access to Deloitte's global UKG community supporting hundreds of clients worldwide, you'll join a genuine community of practice where collaboration, knowledge sharing and continuous learning are central to how we work.

What will your typical day look like?

As a Specialist Lead within our Workforce Payroll Solutions team, you will play a key role delivering UKG Pro Workforce Management (WFM) implementation programs for leading organisations.

You'll combine deep product expertise with consulting capability to design and deliver workforce technology solutions that integrate seamlessly within a client's broader HR and payroll technology landscape.

Your responsibilities will include:

Designing and architecting UKG Pro WFM solutions aligned to client business needs
Facilitating client workshops to capture and translate workforce management requirements
Advising on best use of UKG functionality, integrations and user experience
Configuring and supporting the implementation of UKG WFM modules
Guiding clients through complex workforce design decisions
Collaborating with project teams to deliver solutions aligned to project timelines
Mentoring team members and contributing to capability development across the UKG practice
Staying up to date with the latest UKG releases, innovations and certifications

Our teams typically work across one to three projects at a time, allowing you to focus on delivering meaningful outcomes and high-quality solutions for clients.

Purpose & Impact

Our work combines technology, business insight and practical delivery to help organisations transform how they manage and support their workforce.

In this role, you will:

Deliver complex UKG implementation programs that improve workforce operations and employee experience
Work with leading organisations across health, retail, supply chain, hospitality and government
