AEMO at the Heart of Energy

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this.

We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection.

 Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector!

 About the Team & the Position

 AEMO is embarking on a technology uplift program, designed to modernise systems, enhance operational efficiency, and shape the future of the energy landscape. This large-scale transformation is an exciting opportunity to join a high-profile initiative and contribute to its success from the ground up.

 The Benefits Realisation Lead role is responsible for measuring and tracking agreed outcomes to ensure the program’s goals translate into measurable outcomes. You’ll monitor progress, report on benefits and influence change management across a complex, multi-disciplinary environment. This role will track success measures and validate achievements against Program milestones that will deliver a significant risk reduction Program of national significance.

 Position Responsibilities

The Benefits Realisation Lead is responsible for ensuring all the ‘critical project’ benefits are clearly defined, accurately tracked, and transparently reported in alignment with the Commonwealth Grant Agreement. Working closely with the Program Director, Finance Business Partner, PMO Manager, Program Manager, and Workstream Leads, this role provides end‑to‑end oversight of benefits management across the Program. Key responsibilities include:

Maintaining and reporting on the project Benefits Realisation Management Plan, ensuring the documentation remains current and aligned with the Program requirements

Tracking and monitoring benefits realisation across this critical project in accordance with agreed schedules and established benefit realisation pathways outlined in the consolidated benefit profiles.

Defining frameworks for tracking and reporting project outcomes against benefits.  Conducting pre-and-post implementation evaluations and lessons learnt exercises for benefits reporting and management.

Co-ordination with Program Manager, Workstream leads and Benefits Owners to ensure accurate understanding of the workstreams/projects' contribution to associated benefits and sub-benefits.

Supporting Workstream Leads in completing benefits reporting through the Benefits Register and related mechanisms.

Reviewing benefit updates via the benefit dashboards and preparing monthly benefits reports, highlighting risks to benefit realisation and recommending mitigation actions where required.

Providing input into scope changes and assessing their impact on proposed benefits. Operating within established governance arrangements to ensure robust project management and integration of benefits management.

Preparing high-quality monthly benefit reports, briefing notes, presentations, and progress updates for leadership teams.

Co-ordination with Workstream leads, Project Managers and Benefit Owners to identify new or emergent benefits that warrant tracking. .

 Position Requirements

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in a similar role. Experience in Australian Energy / Utility industry (preferred)

Experience in project or program assurance including deliverable reviews, compliance, and post implementation reviews across complex programs

Well-developed conceptual and analytical skills to identify problems and deliver practical solutions

Strong understanding of governance and associated management frameworks

High proficiency with Microsoft tools and records management systems.

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to guide team towards planned quality outcomes. Ability to build trusted relationships with Leadership, project managers, PMO and governance bodies

High attention to detail, demonstrate critical thinking and problem-solving skills

Strong risk awareness, able to identify emerging issues and escalate appropriately.

Must be an Australian citizen with the ability to attain and maintain an appropriate level of national security clearance.

Education/ professional Certifications: 

Tertiary and/or post graduate qualifications in Business Administration, Project Management, or related field.

 In return some of our benefits to you

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more.

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility).

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).

About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.





About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Logistics Support Analysis Lead to join the AIR6500 team, supporting the delivery of Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) and Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) activities within an Agile program environment.

In this role, you will work closely with the ILS Lead and Systems Engineering, Integration and Test (SEIT) Team, to:

Plan and lead the team to execute Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) work packages within the Program’s Agile framework.

Develop technical LSA studies and evaluations of engineering design concepts.

Lead and Mentor the team tasked to capture LSA data in associated templates and databases.

Develop and compile plans, studies and reports as required contractually.

Lead workshops with internal and external stakeholders to define and tailor the LSA program.

Ensure compliance with standards, certification and assurance to support system design certification.

Serve as SME on LSA activities, providing advice and guidance to internal and external stakeholders.

 

About you:

You are an experienced LSA professional who has a deep knowledge of stakeholder management and ability to identify, analyse, select, document and design for all LSA requirements. You bring:

Deep knowledge of ILS process, procedures and the overall engineering product lifecycle.

Defence Industry Project delivery experience including, specialist technical application of Integrated Logistics Support including Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) tasks, sparing, maintenance support, training and documentation to engineering projects.

Extensive experience with supportability analysis, MRD, and Repairable Item Management.

Leadership of large teams to deliver successful outcomes.

Effective technical guidance, mentoring and training to other employees.

Deep understanding of working with and leading contractual processes and deliverables.


Desirable:

Knowledge of adjacent ILS disciplines including training and publications.

Experience using tools such as MILIS/ERP, CAMM2, OPUS10, Eagle or similar logistics/reliability systems.

Experience in Agile development application.


Why join us:

Contribute to a nationally critical Joint Air Battle Management capability

Work within a complex, multi-domain Defence system-of-systems program

Collaborate with Australian and US teams on advanced Defence technologies

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.
